sections.title: Part 2
sections.passage: You will hear a speaker giving information about festival workshops for children and their families.

questionGroups.instructions: What information is given about each of the following festival workshops? Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–H, next to Questions 11–16.

options.text: involves painting and drawing
will be led by a prize-winning author
is aimed at children with a disability
involves a drama activity
focuses on new relationships
is aimed at a specific age group
explores an unhappy feeling
raises awareness of a particular culture

questionGroups.question: Which TWO reasons does the speaker give for recommending Alive and Kicking?

options.text: It will appeal to both boys and girls.
The author is well known.
It has colourful illustrations.
It is funny.
It deals with an important topic.

questionGroups.question: Which TWO pieces of advice does the speaker give to parents about reading?

options.text: Encourage children to write down new vocabulary.
Allow children to listen to audio books.
Get recommendations from librarians.
Give children a choice about what they read.
Only read aloud to children until they can read independently.

sections.title: Part 3
sections.passage: You will hear two teachers, Clare and Jake, discussing a science experiment for Year 12 students.

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: How does Clare feel about the students in her Year 12 science class?

options.text: worried that they are not making progress
challenged by their poor behaviour in class
frustrated at their lack of interest in the subject

questions.question: How does Jake react to Clare's suggestion about an experiment based on children's diet?

options.text: He is concerned that the results might not be meaningful.
He feels some of the data might be difficult to obtain.
He suspects that the conclusions might be upsetting.

questions.question: What problem do they agree may be involved in an experiment involving animals?

options.text: Any results may not apply to humans.
It may be complicated to get permission.
Students may not be happy about animal experiments.

questions.question: What question do they decide the experiment should address?

options.text: Are mice capable of controlling their food intake?
Does an increase in sugar lead to health problems?
How much do supplements of different kinds affect health?

questions.question: Clare might also consider doing another experiment involving

options.text: other types of food supplement.
different genetic strains of mice.
varying amounts of exercise.

questionGroups.instructions: Complete the flowchart below. Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–H, next to Questions 26–30.

options.text: size
escape
age
water
cereal
calculations
changes
colour

questions.question: Choose mice which are all the same ______
Divide the mice into two groups, each with a different ______
Feed group B the same, but also sugar contained in ______
Place them in a weighing chamber to prevent ______

sections.title: Part 4
sections.passage: You will hear a lecture about microplastics and their effects.

questionGroups.title: Microplastics
questionGroups.instructions: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

content.text:
**Where microplastics come from**
● fibres from some

during washing
● the breakdown of large pieces of plastic
● waste from industry
● the action of vehicle tyres on roads

**Effects of microplastics**
● They cause injuries to the

content.question: They cause injuries to the ______ of wildlife

content.text:
of wildlife and affect their digestive systems.
● They enter the food chain, e.g., in bottled and tap water,

content.question: They enter the food chain, e.g., in bottled and tap water, ______ and seafood

content.text:
and seafood.
● They may not affect human health, but they are already banned in skin cleaning products and

content.question: already banned in skin cleaning products and ______ in some countries

content.text:
in some countries.
● Microplastics enter the soil through the air, rain and

content.question: Microplastics enter the soil through the air, rain and ______

content.text:
.

**Microplastics in the soil – a study by Anglia Ruskin University**
● Earthworms are important because they add

content.question: Earthworms are important because they add ______ to the soil

content.text:
to the soil.
● The study aimed to find whether microplastics in earthworms affect the

of plants.
● The study found that microplastics caused
   -

loss in earthworms
   - fewer seeds to germinate
   - a rise in the level of

content.question: a rise in the level of ______ in the soil

content.text:
in the soil.

**The study concluded:**
- soil should be seen as an important natural process.
- changes to soil damage both ecosystems and

content.question: changes to soil damage both ecosystems and ______
